danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct car models with visible damage states, opposing race vehicles on track
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats to complete objective; wrecking them is primary goal

danger_signals.visual_form: Human figures walking/standing in environment across city, mine, and industrial areas
danger_signals.meaning: Killable targets that extend race completion time; represent chaos/danger of arena

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles in city, mine, and industrial settings
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that can damage player vehicle or slow progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer display showing remaining time to complete race
danger_signals.meaning: Urgency signal; depletion means race failure unless time bonuses collected

safety_signals.meaning: Resources that extend race time and provide survival mechanism

safety_signals.visual_form: Finish line or designated course endpoints
safety_signals.meaning: Safe states; reaching finish completes race objective

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Computer-controlled competitor cars with distinct visual designs and damage models
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player car appears in open world across eleven different locations (city, mine, industrial areas)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic civilians, no faction differentiation documented
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present across urban and industrial environments; represent civilian/neutral faction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Competitor vehicles visible on track with varying damage states; pedestrians active in environment; timer running
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Computer AI competitors drive toward race objectives; pedestrians walk/stand in paths; player must navigate while attacking or avoiding

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple vehicle wrecks visible, environmental damage from collisions, pedestrian casualties marked on landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Competitors become damaged and slower; pedestrians flee or remain stationary; world state reflects escalating chaos

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer display prominently shows low remaining time; bonus items glow or flash on track
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must aggressively pursue time-extending actions (damage opponents, kill pedestrians, collect bonuses) to survive

environmental_storytelling.example: Eleven distinct locations (city, mine, industrial areas) communicate setting through visual environment design; each location has unique architectural and environmental hazards that communicate world identity
Player vehicle 'Die Anna' and competing vehicles have distinct visual models that communicate faction/competitor identity through design language
Accumulated vehicle damage, pedestrian casualties, and environmental destruction visually communicate the chaotic destruction derby nature of the world state
Track layouts across city streets, mining operations, and industrial zones communicate lawlessness—public spaces converted into combat arenas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Race toward objectives while avoiding player and other competitors; display visible damage accumulation
Walk or stand in environment; available as targets for player actions
Visual deformation visible on car model; handling and performance presumably degraded (gameplay impact)

notes: Carmageddon communicates world-state primarily through destruction progression and time-based urgency. The game lacks traditional faction signaling (no aligned NPCs or faction territories) but instead establishes competition through direct vehicle opposition. Visual communication relies heavily on: (1) environmental variety across 11 locations establishing distinct arenas, (2) vehicle damage states showing combat progression, (3) pedestrian presence establishing moral/chaos dimension, (4) timer as constant danger signal. The paradigm is anarchic destruction racing rather than politically-motivated conflict, so faction indicators are minimal. Safety signals are sparse—the game emphasizes danger through resource scarcity (time) rather than safe zones.
